hero_badge: Open source · MIT · A Montte product
hero_title: One typed boundary for every signature.
hero_subhead: A1/PKCS#12, PDF/PAdES, XML-DSig, and remote providers behind one Effect-native seam. Swap the signer, keep the program — browser or server, typed errors, Redacted secrets.
signer_title: Sign a real PDF, right here.
signer_lead: No backend, no upload, no SaaS. Drop in a PDF, click where the signature goes, load your A1 (e-CPF / e-CNPJ), and sign with WebCrypto. The document and the private key never leave the page.
signer_step1_body: Rendered locally with pdf.js. The file never leaves the page.
signer_step2_body: Drop the PAdES signature rectangle anywhere on any page.
signer_step3_body: Your .pfx and password sign in-browser via WebCrypto.
showcase_lead: Eight signature providers behind one typed boundary. Pick a provider, keep the same request shape — the create*SignatureRequest call swaps, your application code doesn't.
showcase_feature_adapters_desc: Every provider is one create*SignatureRequest over a shared SignatureHttpClient — provider-specific code stays out of your app.
showcase_feature_errors_desc: Nothing throws. Every failure is one SignatureKitError on the Effect channel, tagged with the provider and a literal code.
showcase_feature_redacted_desc: Tokens and API keys stay wrapped in Redacted until the HTTP boundary — never logged, never leaked.
showcase_feature_shape_desc: The same title / documents / recipients request normalizes to a RemoteSignatureRequest across all eight providers.
showcase_card_hint: Use arrows or click a logo
cap_lead: A1 / PKCS#12 in the browser or on the server, PDF·PAdES and XML-DSig, eight remote providers, one typed error channel, and a React surface for in-browser signing — the same Effect-native ergonomics across every backend.
cap1_desc: inspect, sign, and verify hang off one Signatures service — provide a layer and the same three accessors drive every backend.
cap2_title: A1 in the browser or on the server
cap2_desc: Load an e-CPF / e-CNPJ A1 (.pfx / PKCS#12) once for a Signatures layer; same call server or browser, password kept in Redacted.
cap3_desc: signPdf seals under a PAdES policy: pades-icp-brasil for the Brazilian ICP chain, or pades-ades.
cap4_desc: signXml writes an enveloped XML-DSig over a referenced node; verifyXml needs no layer — the key rides in the document.
cap5_desc: DocuSign, Clicksign, Assinafy, ZapSign, DocuSeal, Adobe Acrobat Sign, Dropbox Sign, and Documenso each get a create*SignatureRequest over one HTTP client.
cap6_desc: Nothing throws. Every failure is one SignatureKitError carrying one of 18 literal codes, caught in the Effect error channel.
cap7_title: Sign in the browser, with React
cap7_desc: @signature-kit/react brings a type-safe signature builder, a canvas ink pad, and in-browser A1 signing. The live signer above runs the whole flow client-side.
gs_title: Your first signature, end to end.
gs_lead: Install the core seam and the A1 signer, then construct one layer and run a program that inspects the certificate and signs.
gs_note1: carries the typed seam. Each signer and format installs only when you reach for it:
gs_note2_c: . From here, swap the A1 layer for a format —
gs_note2_e: — or hand the seam to a remote provider.
sponsors_lead: Built by Montte and running in production at Licitei — one of Brazil's largest procurement platforms — where it signs thousands of bid documents in the browser.
final_lead: Open source, MIT licensed, Effect-native. One typed seam for A1, PDF, XML, and remote providers — every failure a value you can match on.
footer_brand_blurb: One typed boundary for every signature — A1/PKCS#12, PDF/PAdES, XML-DSig, and remote providers behind a single Effect-native seam. Open source, MIT licensed.
signer_dialog_desc: Place each signature, load your A1, and sign — all locally with WebCrypto. Nothing is uploaded.
signer_dropzone: Drop PDFs to sign — they stay in your browser
signer_aria_page_placement: Page {page}: press Enter to place the signature at the center, then use the arrow keys to move it. You can also click anywhere on the page.
signer_status_placed: Signature placed. Move to the next document, or add your A1 certificate.
signer_status_click_to_place: Click the page to drop the signature box on each document.
signer_status_cert_loaded: Certificate loaded in browser memory. Enter the password.
signer_status_signing: Signing in your browser with WebCrypto…
signer_status_signed: Signed. Download the PDFs and verify them in any PAdES reader.
signer_step1_hint_empty: Upload one or more PDFs to start.
signer_step1_hint_place: Click the page to drop the signature box. Keyboard: focus it, Enter to place, arrows to nudge.
signer_step1_hint_some: Some documents still need a signature — pick them above to place it.
signer_step1_hint_all: All documents placed. Continue to your A1 certificate.
signer_step2_note_a: One certificate signs the whole batch. The .pfx stays in local memory and the password is wrapped in
signer_step3_intro: Drawn onto the page before signing. Full mark on the signed page; initials on every other page when repeated.
signer_draw_hint: Used full-size on the signed page.
signer_cert_hint_continue: Press Continue in Step 2 to read your name from the certificate.
signer_cert_hint_add: Add your A1 in Step 2 — this fills in from the certificate.
signer_cert_initials_label: From your certificate — no typing. Initials:
signer_rubric_note_draw: Repeat your drawn mark on every other page — still one signature.
signer_rubric_note_initials: Repeat your initials on every other page — still one signature.
signer_skip_note: Unplaced documents that will be skipped: {count}
signer_validate_note: Opens the official ICP-Brasil validator — upload a downloaded PDF there for the conformance report.
signer_err_decode: Failed to decode the drawn signature.
signer_err_dimensions: Missing dimensions for the placed page.
signer_err_none_prepared: No documents could be prepared for signing.
signer_place_done: Placed on {count} {noun}. Nudge any with a click or the arrow keys.
signer_place_none: Couldn't place automatically — click each page to position the signature.
autosign_title: Position once. Sign the whole stack.
autosign_lead: Several real contracts are generated right in your browser with @react-pdf/renderer, rendered on the same pdf.js placement canvas the signer uses, then auto-placed and signed in a queue — one document at a time.
autosign_note: The cryptographic CMS step is illustrative here, but the visible signature stamp is baked onto the real PDF bytes — the same bytes you could download. For real PAdES signing with your own certificate, use the live signer above.
